</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The blocks are still merged (id=13, 100 pixels, spanning rows 34-43). UP didn't split them. So DOWN merges but UP doesn't split.
Let me reconsider the game mechanics. Maybe the game is about building the pattern from scratch:
1. Select a color
2. SPACE paints the block below
3. DOWN merges adjacent same-color blocks
The reference shows white on top, purple on bottom. Currently I have a merged white block. I need to somehow create a purple block below.
Maybe I should:
1. Select purple
2. SPACE - this would paint the merged block purple? Or just the top part?
Let me try selecting purple and pressing SPACE to see what happens.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the whole game. Looking at the reference panel:
- White block on top (rows 1-7, cols 1-10)
- Purple block on bottom (rows 8-14, cols 1-10)
The main area has:
- Bordered block (selectable/rotatable)
- Blocks below to paint
The goal might be to make the blocks below the bordered block match the reference pattern. Currently the merged block is all white. I need it to be white on top and purple on bottom.
Let me try selecting purple and pressing SPACE. If SPACE paints the entire merged block, then I'd need to split it first. But if SPACE only paints the top portion, that could work.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try selecting purple and pressing SPACE on the merged white block
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 4, 'col': 43}, 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
